notes: Thirtieth validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Official materials say the school was formerly known as Healthy Start Academy.
Its mission says the school exists to build a world-class K-12 school in Durham that empowers students to thrive in college, career, and life.
The school says its vision includes an Early College for All pathway through Durham Technical Community College.
Official materials identify it as North Carolina's first charter school.
Team pages list Alex Quigley as Executive Director.
Board pages list Elizabeth Gnatek-Morey as Board Chair.
Its mission says the school offers a stable, emotionally supportive educational environment that empowers children to achieve their true academic and social potential.
Official materials say the school gives priority to students connected to foster care or the child welfare system.
The school describes its approach as serving the whole child, with equal attention to academics and social-emotional wellbeing.
Official pages say it opened on Crossnore's Winston-Salem campus in August 2025.
Public leadership materials identify Rashawn Meekins as principal.
The school describes its learning model as a mix of direct, interactive, experiential, and independent learning.
Its public branding uses the line Home of the Wildcats.
Official materials show the school highlights character traits such as citizenship in community projects.
The school's wellness policy says every student receives at least thirty minutes of moderate to vigorous physical activity each day.
Official pages emphasize a strong board-governed structure with published meeting schedules, minutes, and election information.
The staff directory lists Jessica Poole as principal.
Recent school messaging refers to families and students as Wildcats on the prowl.
Its published slogan is Inspiring lifelong leaders.
Official materials say the academic model culminates with students maximizing NC Career and College Promise through Central Carolina Community College.
The school says it opened in the 2022-2023 school year in a renovated former Pantry and Circle K corporate facility.
Its mission says the school establishes and maintains a challenging learning environment that respects individual learners and prepares students to succeed in a globally competitive society.
Staff pages list Greg Batten as principal.
Board pages list Dr. Don Sloan as president.
Its mission says the school helps children of diverse backgrounds learn and grow at their own pace in a Montessori environment that models grace and courtesy.
Official history says the private preschool opened in 2012 and the public charter school followed in 2013.
The school highlights a Building Hope IMPACT Award for Community Engagement.
Its strategic plan says graduates should become confident, kind, and responsible citizens of the world.
Board materials identify Nicole Smith as President of the charter board.
Official materials repeatedly describe the school as a peaceful community of lifelong learners.
